title: 'Unhe Fortuner chahiye thi': Kin claim Noida woman beaten, thrown off terrace over dowry
content: A 24-year-old woman in Greater Noida allegedly died after being thrown from her house terrace, with her family accusing her husband and in-laws of dowry harassment. The family claims she was beaten and injured with a sharp object before being pushed to stage an accident. Police are investigating the incident.

title: Pakistan deploys squadron of JF-17 jets, 8,000 troops to Saudi Arabia amid US-Iran war
content: Pakistan has deployed 8,000 troops, fighter jets, and an air defense system to Saudi Arabia under a mutual defense pact. This substantial, combat-capable force aims to support the kingdom amid rising regional tensions. The move follows Iran's submission of a revised peace proposal through Pakistan, as a fragile ceasefire with the US remains under strain.

title: Parliamentry panel summons NTA chief after NEET paper leak; reform in focus
content: ​NTA chairperson Pradeep Kumar Joshi and other bureaucrats have been summoned for deliberations on May 21. The Parliamentary Standing Committee on Education, Women, Children, Youth and Sports will seek the views of top education ministry bureaucrats on the alleged paper leak and reforms of NTA, which holds centralised admission tests to various undergraduate courses, on May 21, a Rajya Sabha notice said according to PTI.

title: 'Bail is the rule, jail the exception': What SC's UAPA ruling means for Umar Khalid
content: In a landmark ruling, the Supreme Court underscored the primacy of bail, regardless of UAPA constraints, asserting that personal freedom must not be persistently restricted. By referencing the KA Najeeb case, the justices highlighted that extended detention without trial contravenes constitutional principles, challenging previous judgments that had sidestepped this fundamental right.

title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei makes a 'doomsday' prediction for software companies
content: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei told WSJ at the World Economic Forum in Davos that software is heading toward being "cheap, maybe essentially free," warning that whole careers built around writing code may not survive the AI shift. The Anthropic chief executive also predicted SaaS incumbents leaning on code complexity as a moat could "go bust" as ServiceNow, Snowflake and Microsoft stocks slide and Anthropic eyes a $900 billion valuation.

title: First look: Indian Railways reveals proposed bullet train design
content: India's first bullet train, destined for the Mumbai-Ahmedabad route, has had its design unveiled by Indian Railways. This ambitious project, spanning 508 km, is progressing with station foundation work completed in Gujarat and ongoing construction in Maharashtra, including an undersea tunnel. The initiative aims to boost indigenous high-speed rail capabilities.

title: Suvendu govt ends religion-based schemes in Bengal, scraps state OBC list
content: The BJP government has also done away with the exisiting state OBC list in accordance with the Calcutta high court judgement and would set up a panel to decide quota eligibility. Last year on July 28, however, the CJI-led bench of the Supreme Court had vacated high court's stay on implementation of the new OBC list that had a total of 140 sub-groups under OBC-A and OBC-B categories, including 80 belonging to the Muslim community.

title: 'BCCI not under RTI Act’: CIC warns superimposed govt control could disrupt economic structure
content: The Central Information Commission ruled the BCCI is not under the RTI Act, dismissing an appeal seeking information on its national representation and player selection. The CIC noted that increased government oversight could disrupt BCCI's finely balanced economic structure, citing its financial independence and market-driven operations, particularly the IPL's success.

title: Salesforce CEO who halted hiring sees company spending millions on Anthropic tokens
content: Salesforce is significantly boosting AI spending, projecting hundreds of millions on Anthropic tokens, while halting software engineer hiring. CEO Marc Benioff highlights AI's role in enhancing engineer productivity by over 30%, enabling faster output and lower development costs. This strategic shift reflects a broader tech trend of leveraging AI for efficiency, with humans still integral to operations.

title: 'Balanced probe?' Twisha's father questions police probe; cash reward announced for husband
content: Family members of a 33-year-old woman found dead in Bhopal protested, demanding a fresh post-mortem at AIIMS Delhi. Police formed an SIT to investigate dowry harassment allegations against her husband and mother-in-law, a retired judge who received anticipatory bail. The family alleges murder and criticizes the handling of the case.

title: Elon Musk 'repeats' his woke mind virus vow; says: It killed my son, I want to destroy it
content: Elon Musk reiterated his vow to "destroy the woke mind virus," claiming it led to his son's transition and that he was deceived into approving puberty blockers. Musk's estranged daughter, Vivian Jenna Wilson, has publicly refuted his claims about her childhood and his motivations.

title: Panchayat Season 5 locked for OTT release: When and where to watch
content: Mark your calendars, rural drama aficionados! 'Panchayat' Season 5 is officially on track for a 2026 launch on Amazon Prime Video. Join Abhishek Tripathi as he battles through the complexities of life in Phulera, engaging with unresolved politics and deepening interpersonal ties. With unexpected turns ahead, you won't want to miss how Abhishek faces a new set of hurdles!

title: Roger Federer and Mirka Federer combined net worth 2026
content: By 2026, Roger Federer and Mirka Federer’s combined net worth will be one of the largest financial empires among athletes in sports history. The wealth has been built up by ATP prize money, record-breaking endorsement deals and strategic investments like On Running, showing the enduring brand power of tennis. Lifestyle management, family operations, and business direction are also big players in the game, along with Roger Federer and Mirka Federer.

title: Andre Agassi and Steffi Graf combined net worth 2026
content: Steffi Graf and Andre Agassi are a very recognizable tennis pair, and they have an estimated net worth in 2026 of $290 million. They’ve become wealthy by winning Grand Slams, signing multiyear contracts and other investments. Both benefited from the brand equity, business pursuits and financial planning that they developed during and after their successful professional tennis careers.

title: FIFA 2026 World Cup row erupts as fans demand BTS removal from Jungkook song
content: Fans are criticizing BigHit Music after BTS was reportedly credited on Jungkook’s solo FIFA World Cup song Dreamers. Many argued that Jungkook alone created and performed the track, calling the move unfair to his solo achievements. The backlash grew amid fresh buzz about BTS potentially performing at the 2026 FIFA World Cup halftime show, reigniting conversations about solo recognition, group branding, and BigHit’s marketing strategy surrounding Jungkook’s global success.

title: Inside India’s suicide crisis, where metros lead in deaths of despair
content: India's major cities face alarming mental health strain, with Delhi, Bengaluru, and Mumbai reporting high suicide numbers and rates according to NCRB 2024 data. Family stress, unemployment, and emotional pressures are identified as leading causes, highlighting an urgent need for improved mental health infrastructure and support systems.

title: ‘Smiling Buddha’ at 52: Test that announced India’s nuclear arrival to the world
content: Fifty-two years ago, India detonated a nuclear device in Rajasthan's Thar desert, codenamed 'Smiling Buddha'. This event, coinciding with Buddha Purnima, marked India's entry into the nuclear club. Despite being officially termed a 'peaceful nuclear explosion', the test ignited debate and reshaped India's global standing, demonstrating scientific self-reliance and announcing strategic intent.

title: Who is Aaron Rai’s golfer wife Gaurika Bishnoi? All about PGA Champion’s personal life
content: Aaron Rai’s PGA Championship victory brought fresh attention to his relationship with fellow professional golfer Gaurika Bishnoi. Bishnoi, an accomplished Indian golfer with eight wins on the Hero Women’s Pro Golf Tour, has become an important part of Rai’s personal and professional journey. Rai openly credited his wife for helping him through the pressure of major championship golf, while Bishnoi’s supportive social media posts and tournament appearances highlighted the close bond the pair share within the sport.

title: 'Son-in-law a drug addict': Twisha's father makes big claim, seeks fresh autopsy in dowry death case
content: The father of Twisha Sharma, found dead at her in-laws' home, has demanded a fresh post-mortem at AIIMS-Delhi, alleging a cover-up and refuting claims of drug use by his daughter. He suspects murder and calls for an independent investigation, criticizing the anticipatory bail granted to his daughter's mother-in-law.

title: Energy security first: India says buying Russian oil irrespective of US waiver
content: Addressing a media briefing on Monday, Sharma said India had been sourcing oil from Russia before the waiver period, during it, and continues to do so now as well.

title: ‘If they don’t agree, we have other methods’: CM Yogi Adityanath on namaz on UP roads
content: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath stated that offering namaz on public roads is not permissible, as it disrupts traffic and inconveniences the public. He emphasized that roads are for movement and suggested organized religious practices in designated places or in shifts to maintain public order and normal life.

title: ‘Peddi’ trailer: Ram Charan fights for his people through sports and spirit
content: Ram Charan and Janhvi Kapoor star in the upcoming sports action drama 'Peddi,' directed by Buchi Babu Sana. The recently released trailer showcases Ram Charan as a revered athlete, a 'deity on the playground,' who fights oppression through sports. The film, featuring a stellar cast and AR Rahman's music, hits theaters on June 4.

title: How fighter pilots eject in emergencies; veterans who 'pulled the handle' recount experience
content: A mid-air collision of two US Navy Growlers during an air show highlights the extreme dangers of low-level flying.  Despite advanced ejection seat technology, which successfully saved four pilots in this incident, the risks to pilots remain immense.  Retired Air Marshals emphasize the critical need for quick decisions and the traumatic, yet life-saving, nature of ejections.

title: Pilot still holding controls: Man who entered Air India crash morgue recounts chilling scenes
content: Chilling details emerge from the Air India AI-171 crash mortuary, with a witness describing horrific scenes. The captain's body was found seated, still clutching the controls, suggesting a desperate attempt to save the aircraft. Investigations into pilot error versus technical failure continue, with conflicting timelines and CCTV footage raising new questions.

title: Kuwaiti champion refuses Israeli rival handshake at Abu Dhabi Jiu-Jitsu event
content: The under-77 kg division at the Abu Dhabi Grand Slam ended in controversy after Israeli jiu-jitsu athlete Yoav Manor secured a bronze medal on Sunday. While Manor celebrated one of the biggest achievements of his young career, the medal ceremony quickly turned tense when Kuwaiti fighter Jassim Alhatem reportedly refused to shake his hand or stand beside him for the traditional podium photograph.

title: ‘Fit lag raha hu na’: Dhoni trains with NSG as CSK fans wait for Chepauk return
content: MS Dhoni's visit to the NSG Hub, showcasing his fitness and shooting skills, coincides with a high-stakes IPL 2026 match for CSK. With Dhoni sidelined by injury and speculation rife about his final Chepauk appearance, fans anticipate an emotional farewell, regardless of his playing status. The team's playoff hopes hinge on upcoming wins.

title: Honda City facelift launch this week: Top things to know
content: Mechanically, it’s expected to remain the same and will continue to get two engine options - a 1.5-litre, 4-cylinder, NA petrol engine that puts out 121bhp and 145Nm of torque and comes paired either with a 6-speed MT or a CVT gearbox. There’s also a segment-first strong hybrid powertrain offer.

title: Kerala CM forms SIT to probe 2023 attacks on Congress workers during Left regime
content: A fierce political faceoff had ensued three years back during the Left regime when Congress workers were thrashed for waving black flags at Vijayan's motorcade, leading to an intense war of words between two rival factions in the coastal state. Calling Vijayan Kerala's "most cruel" home minister, senior Congress leader MM Hassan had alleged that Youth Congress protesters were beaten up for waving black flags at the CM's motorcade.

title: Punjab minister Sanjeev Arora sent to judicial custody in Rs 100 crore money laundering case
content: Punjab's Industries Minister Sanjeev Arora has been taken into judicial custody following his arrest by the Enforcement Directorate in a significant money laundering scandal involving ₹100 crore tied to fraudulent GST billings. During a rare family visit, his loved ones met him for the first time since his detainment.

title: ‘Above my pay grade’: DC coach Badani questions CA’s decision to release Starc late
content: Delhi Capitals kept their IPL 2026 playoff hopes alive with a win, but player availability, especially Mitchell Starc's, remains a season-long concern. Coach Hemang Badani highlighted the challenges of overseas player access due to national board decisions. Starc's match-winning four-wicket haul proved his value, earning him praise for professionalism and mentorship.

title: West Bengal cabinet's big decisions: Free bus travel for women, 'Annapurna' scheme approved
content: West Bengal's new cabinet, led by chief minister Suvendu Adhikari, has decided to end religious-based government aid from June.  A new 'Annapurna' scheme will provide Rs 3,000 monthly to eligible women, who will also get free bus travel. The state will also review its OBC list and form a new Pay Commission.

title: ED arrests AAP's Deepak Singla; Atishi alleges bid to access organisational data
content: AAP leader Deepak Singla was arrested by the Enforcement Directorate in a bank fraud case following raids in Delhi and Goa. The party accused the BJP of using central agencies to intimidate workers and acquire organizational data amid AAP's growing popularity in Goa.

title: Ashok Saraf attends Cannes festival for first time at 78, holds wife's hand on red carpet
content: Veteran Marathi actors Ashok Saraf and Nivedita Saraf, along with Prajakta Mali, Ankita Prabhu-Walawalkar, and Kedar Joshi, attended Cannes 2026. At 78, Ashok made his Cannes debut, walking the red carpet in a bandhgala while Nivedita stunned in a Paithani saree. Their appearance won hearts and went viral online.

title: Heatwave alert: IMD forecasts severe heat conditions in northwest and central India this week
content: The northern plains and heart of India prepare for a relentless surge of heat, as Delhi might swelter at a scorching 45°C. The Indian Meteorological Department warns of extreme weather, urging those most at risk to take necessary precautions. Meanwhile, amidst the heatwave, sporadic showers and thunderstorms are forecasted for Jammu & Kashmir, Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh, and adjacent areas.

title: Karnataka Police orders statewide inspection drive to streamline policing
content: ADGPs and IGPs have been assigned districts and commissionerates to review policing standards, administrative efficiency and implementation of new criminal laws between May and July 2026.

title: NCW takes suo motu cognisance of Morbi sexual exploitation case
content: "The NCW has taken suo motu cognisance of extremely serious media reports regarding the alleged sexual exploitation and rape of a woman and her minor daughter in Morbi district of Gujarat," the commission said in a post on X.

title: NEET-UG paper leak: CBI arrests Latur coaching institute owner
content: On May 17, the CBI conducted searches at the main office of Renukai Chemistry Classes (RCC) in Maharashtra’s Latur city; accused Shivaraj Motegaonkar is said to be close to chemistry lecturer P.V. Kulkarni, who is associated with the National Testing Agency (NTA) and was earlier arrested by the CBI

title: Another FIR against Bengaluru businessman in blackmail and harassment case
content: A 50-year-old customs officer has filed a complaint alleging that he and his family received death threats from Kripalani, after the officer imposed penalties in a customs evasion case.

title: Missing teenager found dead in Coimbatore; two minor friends held
content: The boys had gone the Noyyal river near Masaniamman temple to catch fish, after which they had a physical altercation

title: India, Sweden agree to elevate ties to Strategic Partnership
content: During the delegation-level talks, Narendra Modi and Ulf Kristersson reviewed the full spectrum of India-Sweden relations and agreed to elevate the bilateral relationship to a Strategic Partnership

title: Indian Tobacco product exports fall 34.81%; A.P. farmers hit by surplus, taxes
content: Tobacco Board authorised 142 million kg of FCV crop for 2025-26, but Andhra farmers produced 230 million kg; auction prices fall, exports decline 34.81%

title: Tamil Nadu tourist dies after being caught in clash between two elephants at Dubare Elephant Camp in Karnataka
content: Karnataka Forest Minister Eshwar Khandre orders safety review and calls for visitor curbs around elephants

title: V.D. Satheesan sworn in as the new Chief Minister of Kerala
content: Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar administered the oath of office and secrecy to Mr. Satheesan and the Ministers-designate over a one-hour ceremony

title: NIA files probe report against five juvenile persons in Pakistan-linked espionage case
content: The juvenile persons had provided active support in installing spy cameras, and transmitting sensitive information, including photographs and videos with geo-tagging, the investigation report says

title: Chandrababu Naidu congratulates TTD on near 90% forest cover in Tirumala hills
content: Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams reaches 89.40% green cover in Seshachalam Hills; Chief Minister hails effort to protect forest wealth and restore native species

title: PM Modi in Norway: PM Modi thanks Norway for standing with India on terrorism
content: PM Modi will also review the progress made in India-Norway relations and explore avenues to further strengthen them, with a focus on trade and investment, the Ministry of External Affairs said in a statement

title: West Bengal government to roll out Annapurna Bhandar, discontinue schemes based on religious categorisation
content: The approval of Annapurna Bhandar came in the second Cabinet meeting of the BJP government, nine days after the Chief Minister and five other ministers took oath

title: Geopolitics has changed since last India-Nordic Summit, conflicts on agenda for talks: Norwegian envoy
content: As U.S. sanction waivers on Russian oil lapse, oil and gas major exporter Norway could pitch for more energy supplies to India

title: West Bengal CID reopens 59 cases linked to 2021 post-poll violence, 181 fresh FIRs registered
content: "In view of the complaints received relating to post-poll violence in 2021, necessary legal action is being taken in accordance with procedure," says a senior CID officer

title: Parliamentary panel summons NTA chief; to review reforms, NEET-UG paper leak case on May 21
content: The agenda includes a review of the implementation of the K Radhakrishnan Committee report on NTA reforms and an update on the investigation into the alleged NEET-UG paper leak case.

title: "Govt is not just talking but showing through its actions, 96% schools have all facilities": CM Yogi felicitates part-time instructors
content: Yogi Adityanath further said the party is the only political organisation globally that has consistently upheld the principle of “Nation First, Party Second, and Individual Last”

title: UDF government to implement two ‘Indira guarantees’, including free bus travel for women
content: Kerala CM Satheesan announced free bus travel for all women on KSRTC buses and a dedicated department for senior citizens to ensure their dignity and welfare

title: Chandrababu Naidu seeks tax relief for FCV tobacco farmers; writes to Sitharaman, Goyal
content: GST on cigarettes raised from 28% to 40%; excise duty ranges from ₹2,050 to ₹8,500 per 1,000 cigarettes; 43,000 farmers’ livelihoods at risk

title: Calcutta HC grants protection to TMC’s Falta candidate Jahangir Khan from coercive action
content: The court directed that no coercive steps will be taken against Mr. Khan in connection with the pending criminal prosecutions till May 26.

title: Indian market remains resilient despite rising volatility, says SEBI chairperson
content: “There are countries in European Union where they do not have a single IPO in a whole year, whereas we have got 366 last year,” says SEBI chief Tuhin Kanta Pandey.

title: The summer story of Tamil Nadu’s electricity demand
content: Searing summer heat has already caused the electricity demand in Tamil Nadu to touch an all-time high this year, even as power managers struggle to maintain sustained power supply. While extensive infrastructure upgrades and anticipatory power purchase agreements are in place, the reality of a financial crisis in the power sector cannot be wished away, says R. Srikanth

title: KGH gets Centre of Competence for Haemoglobinopathies to serve tribal communities
content: ₹3.77-crore facility is the first in South India to introduce newborn screening within 24 hours of birth, aimed at tribal populations across Araku parliamentary constituency

title: Telangana to launch Mahua flower project to build heritage industry around Ippa
content: Telangana is considering Excise policy changes to allow Mahua spirit and liquor, drawing on models in other States

title: Visakhapatnam invites applications for Social Welfare Hostels for 2026-27 from June 12
content: Pre-matric hostels open to Class 3 to Class 10 students; post-matric hostels for Intermediate to post-graduate level; ₹200 monthly allowance and special coaching offered

title: Control menace of touts at Tiruchendur temple: Vanathi Srinivasan urges CM Vijay
content: Touts were collecting between ₹500 and ₹1,000 from devotees, promising them expedited darshan, she alleged

title: Man seeks action against youth over daughter’s death in Erode
content: His daughter informed him on April 21 that a youth, who had allegedly been in a relationship with her for the past four months, was troubling her

title: 40 arrested in Park Circus violence; will not tolerate attacks on police, says Suvendu
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikary urged the Union Home Ministry to keep the remaining CAPF battalions in the State till the State government can augment its police strength

title: Karnataka-Kerala connection: P.C. Vishnunadh’s induction into V.D. Satheesan cabinet brings cheer to people of Tumari in Sagar
content: P.C. Vishnunadh, who took oath as a Minister in the newly sworn-in Kerala government, is married to Ha.Ma. Kanaka, a Kannada poet and native of Haalkere village in Tumari Gram Panchayat

title: Veteran AIADMK leader Semmalai quits party
content: Speaking to reporters in Salem, Mr. Semmalai, considered a strong supporter of Mr. Palaniswami, said currently, senior leaders are unable to develop the AIADMK; without a clear plan to strengthen the party, it is impossible to save it

title: Road crash in Uttar Pradesh leaves 10 dead after van collides with truck
content: The van was going to Sisaiya from Lakhimpur when the accident took place between Unchgaon and Bharehta villages

title: Indian court tells Apple to 'cooperate' in antitrust case
content: An Indian court has told Apple to “fully cooperate” with ​investigators in an antitrust case related to ‌the iPhone apps market

title: BCCI not a public authority under RTI Act: CIC
content: The Commission dismissed an appeal seeking information regarding the provisions and authority under which BCCI represents India and selects players for national and international cricket tournaments

title: Census 2027: Over 11 lakh households in Maharashtra complete self-enumeration
content: Among districts, Pune recorded the highest number of households completing self-enumeration at 1,53,568, followed by Mumbai Suburban with 1,21,068, Thane with 1,16,512, Mumbai city with 61,295 and Nashik with 52,667

title: DMK will rise like a phoenix, says Stalin
content: Mr. Stalin, while congratulating the new government, urged it to continue the welfare schemes implemented by his government

title: Modi government lacks foresight; did not foresee price rise: Congress
content: "Modi government kept everything hidden until the elections, but once the elections were over, prices of everything increased," Mallikarjun Kharge said

title: PM Modi woos Swedish companies to invest in India, says 'reform express' going at full speed
content: “In the last 12 years, India has been working on the basic mantra of reforms, performance, and transformation. And with the government’s political will, this Reform Express is moving forward at full speed,” Mr. Modi said

title: Watch: V.D. Satheesan: a unique phenomenon in Kerala politics
content: V.D. Satheesan is now the unquestionable leader of Kerala Muslims and Christians, around half of the State’s population; over the past five years, he has carefully crafted his political positions.

title: Case booked against Kalam’s former advisor Ponraj
content: Police said Mr. Ponraj, had spoken in a derogatory manner against women in an interview to a Tamil YouTube channel a few weeks ago.

title: DMK urges Centre to hand over Anaimangalam copper plates to Tamil Nadu
content: “The Government of Tamil Nadu too must, without delay, undertake appropriate efforts in this regard and ensure that they are brought back to their motherland,” said former Minister for Archaeology Thangam Thennarasu

title: 19-year-old man succumbs to stab injuries after girlfriend’s family assaults him in Kovilpatti
content: On Sunday night, the woman, along with her father, uncle, and his friend, went to the bus stand to meet Sanjay. During the conversation, an altercation broke out between the parties, during which her uncle stabbed Sanjay with a knife

title: Sikkim govt rolls out odd-even vehicle restriction norm to save fuel
content: All public transport services will remain operational on Saturdays and Sundays to ensure uninterrupted mobility for the public, an official said

title: Supreme Court refuses to entertain PIL for reviewing wages of priests, temple staff
content: A Bench of Justices Vikram Nath and Sandeep Mehta said that it cannot entertain the petition under Article 32 of the Constitution and those aggrieved can directly approach the Court

title: Jute industry seeks new West Bengal government’s intervention to tide over raw material crisis
content: At least 14 mills across the Hooghly industrial belt have either suspended operations or are facing severe production disruptions, according to IJMA sources

title: Karnataka man kills minor daughter over marriage dispute by pushing her into well
content: According to the police, the accused, identified as Thimmarayappa, opposed a proposal to marry off his minor daughter to one of his wife’s relatives after she turned 18.

title: TMC MLA's son held with firearms in Bengal
content: “Arghya Mondal and the others were found to be in possession of firearms. Two pistols and six mobile phones were seized during the operation,” says the officer

title: Return of Leiden copper plates should spark efforts for further repatriations, say Indian archaeologists
content: Efforts needed to bring back copper plates issued by an 8th-century Pandya ruler from the British Museum; inscriptions on Leiden copper plates record a Chola gift of land to build a Buddhist vihara

title: Chola-era Anaimangalam Plates, in possession of Leiden University since 1862, returned to India
content: The plates will be handed over to the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in New Delhi; the ASI, which functions under the Union Ministry of Culture, will decide whether and where the objects will be exhibited in India.

title: Major Policy Shift In Bengal, No More Welfare Funds For Imams, Temple Priests
content: The previous Trinamool government had announced monthly honorarium for imams, muezzins, and purohits a year after it came to power in the state.The new government led by Suvendu Adhikari maintains that welfare programmes should not be based on religi

title: "She Lost 15 kg, Was Being Harassed": Twisha Sharma's Cousin To NDTV
content: Twisha Sharma was discovered hanging at her husband Samarth Singh's house in Bhopal's Katara Hills area. Samarth, a lawyer, is currently on the run.

title: PM Modi Holds Talks With Norwegian Counterpart On Trade, Green Technology
content: PM Modi will also address the India-Norway Business and Research Summit along with the Norwegian Prime Minister.

title: Twisha Sharma's Parents Got Her Train Ticket For May 15. She Died On May 12
content: Twisha Sharma was set to return to her Noida home three days later, on May 15, her maternal uncle, Saurabh Sharma, told NDTV.

title: 'Pressure Has Started Again': NEET Retest Pulls Students Back To Kota, Sikar
content: The familiar cycle of test series, online classes, revision schedules and 12-hour study days has resumed after the medical entrance exam was cancelled amid the controversy over a paper leak.

title: Meet Tushar Kumar, 23, UK's Youngest Indian-Origin Mayor
content: The Labour councillor joined the Elstree and Borehamwood Town Council in 2023 and previously served in the role of Deputy Mayor.

title: Opinion: Opinion | PNS Hangor: Inside The $5 Billion Submarine Fleet Pakistan Is Buying From China
content: With the commissioning of PNS Hangor, what is no longer open to debate is the proposition that any future India-Pakistan conflict, in terms of force composition, will be a proxy engagement involving Chinese military technology.

title: Tourist Trampled Under Two Fighting Elephants At Karnataka Wildlife Camp
content: Karnataka Forest, Ecology and Environment Minister Eshwar B Khandre has expressed grief over the woman's death.

title: "Gave 11 Lakhs, They Wanted 51": Man Claims Daughter Thrown Off Roof By In-Laws
content: The father, who filed a police complaint, alleged his daughter's in-laws demanded an additional dowry of Rs 50 lakh and a Fortuner car.

title: Video: Anchor Fires AK-47 On Live TV As Iran Gives Weapon Training To Civilians
content: The state television reportedly aired clips of the training sessions, saying they were part of 'public readiness to defend the country'.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex, Nifty Settle Flat After Trump Warns Iran 'Clock Is Ticking'
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent advanced toward US$111 a barrel, after adding almost 8 per cent last week.

content: The family members of the girl and the Namrup Aanchalik Gorkha Students Union alleged that Rahman, taking full advantage of the minor's innocence, dragged her inside his home and committed the heinous crime.

title: 'Trapped, Lonely, Unhappy': Noida Woman Had Asked Her Mother To Take Her Home
content: WhatsApp chats between Twisha and her mother have revealed that she felt 'trapped' in an unhappy marriage and was being mentally harassed.

title: Trump Averages 40-60 Trades Per Day: Wall Street Sees A Sketchy Pattern
content: The White House says there is no conflict. The Trump Organization says the trades are executed by third-party institutions using automated systems.

title: On Camera, Woman Saves 3 Children From Burning Building. She Doesn't Make It
content: The incident took place on the night of May 12 in a residential building and has left the local area shaken.

content: US Iran War News LIVE Updates: Israel launched a massive series of airstrikes on southern Lebanon, despite an extension of the truce between the two countries.

content: The former Pakistani Prime Minister has repeatedly claimed that the United States has conspired with Pakistan's opposition parties to remove him because of his independent foreign policy and refusal to fully align with Washington against Russia and C

title: 10 Killed As Van Collides Head-On With Truck On UP Highway
content: Circle Officer of Dhaurahra, Shamsher Bahadur Singh, said all nine passengers travelling in the van and the driver were killed.
